steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Calculate water volume using total mixture volume
steps.content:
The total volume of the mixture is the sum of the water and isopropanol volumes. Given the total volume is 41 mL and isopropanol is 10 mL, subtract the isopropanol volume from the total: $41 - 10 = 31$.

The answer is 31.
